It is also known to be part of the order Piperales. Further classification includes the family being a part of the clade Magnoliidae, which comprises various flowering plant families. The species was first described by the botanist Akiyo Naoki Nishida in 2007. It is considered an endangered species due to its limited geographical range and the impact of human activities on its habitat.

intro: Thismia abei, also known as the "Queen of the Flower World," is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Thismiaceae family, characterized by its unique, tiny flowers and highly modified roots. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Thismia abei include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Thismia abei is often used to prepare herbal infusions and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
